parts.text:
Find and analyze the current navbar implementation in this codebase. Look in apps/web/ directory for navbar components, layout files, and any navigation-related code. Report:
1. Where the navbar component(s) are located
2. What components/libraries it currently uses
3. What styling approach it uses (Tailwind, CSS modules, etc.)
4. What links/items it currently contains
5. Any relevant files like header, nav, navigation components

The user wants me to find and analyze the navbar implementation in the `apps/web/` directory. Let me start by searching for navbar-related components and files in that directory.

I'll use glob to find files that might be related to navbar/navigation, and also grep to search for navbar-related content.
Let me search more broadly in the apps/web directory for navigation-related components and also look at the layout file.

1: import type { Metadata } from "next";
2: import { Inter, JetBrains_Mono } from "next/font/google";
3: import "./globals.css";
4: import { ThemeProvider } from "@/components/providers/ThemeProvider";
5: 
6: const inter = Inter({
7:   variable: "--font-inter",
8:   subsets: ["latin"],
9:   display: "swap",
10:   weight: ["400", "500", "600", "700", "800"],
11: });
12: 
13: const jetbrains = JetBrains_Mono({
14:   variable: "--font-jetbrains",
15:   subsets: ["latin"],
16:   display: "swap",
17:   weight: ["400", "500"],
18: });
19: 
20: export const metadata: Metadata = {
21:   metadataBase: new URL("https://gunmetalapp.vercel.app"),
22:   title: {
23:     default: "Gunmetal",
24:     template: "%s | Gunmetal",
25:   },
26:   description:
27:     "One local API. Every AI provider. Gunmetal turns your AI subscriptions into a unified endpoint.",
28:   keywords: [
29:     "AI",
30:     "inference",
31:     "local API",
32:     "OpenAI compatible",
33:     "AI gateway",
34:     "model routing",

37:     type: "website",
38:     locale: "en_US",
39:     url: "https://gunmetalapp.vercel.app",
40:     siteName: "Gunmetal",
41:     title: "Gunmetal — One Local API. Every AI Provider.",
42:     description:
43:       "Gunmetal turns your AI subscriptions into a unified endpoint. Route, control, and observe every request.",
44:   },
45:   twitter: {
46:     card: "summary_large_image",
47:     title: "Gunmetal — One Local API. Every AI Provider.",
48:     description:
49:       "Gunmetal turns your AI subscriptions into a unified endpoint. Route, control, and observe every request.",
50:   },
51:   icons: {
52:     icon: "/favicon.ico",
53:   },
54: };
55: 
56: export default function RootLayout({
57:   children,
58: }: Readonly<{
59:   children: React.ReactNode;
60: }>) {
61:   return (
62:     <html lang="en" data-theme="dark">
63:       <body className={`${inter.variable} ${jetbrains.variable}`}>
64:         <ThemeProvider>{children}</ThemeProvider>
65:       </body>
66:     </html>
67:   );
68: }

(End of file - total 68 lines)

10: export const moduleCards = [
11:   {
12:     title: "Daemon + Service",
13:     body: "Owns the local API, autostart, port binding, health, and request flow. Other apps talk to this, not your upstream accounts.",
14:   },
15:   {
16:     title: "CLI",
17:     body: "Handles provider auth, keys, models, request history, start, stop, and scripted workflows from one binary.",
18:   },
19:   {
20:     title: "TUI",
21:     body: "Runs as a real operator surface: connect providers, auth them, sync models, mint or revoke keys, inspect snippets, and drill into request history.",
22:   },
23:   {
24:     title: "Providers",
25:     body: "Explicit adapters for subscription providers, gateways, and direct API-key providers with strict model prefixes.",
26:   },
27:   {
28:     title: "Keys + Auth",
29:     body: "Creates Gunmetal-local keys with scopes, provider limits, expiry, disable, revoke, and local-file auth storage.",
30:   },
31:   {
32:     title: "Registry + Telemetry",
33:     body: "Keeps a unified model catalog and lightweight local request history by default, with payload logging left opt-in.",
34:   },
35: ];
36:

38:   {
39:     title: "Subscription",
40:     body: "Bring the plans you already pay for.",
41:     items: ["codex", "copilot"],
42:   },
43:   {
44:     title: "Gateways",
45:     body: "Route through hosted model routers when you want breadth.",
46:     items: ["openrouter", "zen"],
47:   },
48:   {
49:     title: "Direct API Key",
50:     body: "Use standard provider keys under the same local surface.",

52:   },
53: ];
54: 
55: export const docsSections = [
56:   {
57:     title: "Core contract",
58:     body: "Gunmetal issues Gunmetal keys. They work only because requests go to Gunmetal. The upstream provider never sees a fake Gunmetal-issued OpenAI or Copilot key.",
59:   },
60:   {
61:     title: "Gateway semantics",
62:     body: "Gunmetal is a normalized gateway by default. Use passthrough only when you explicitly request provider-native behavior through gunmetal.mode and provider_options.",
63:   },
64:   {
65:     title: "Model naming",
66:     body: "Every model is provider-prefixed. Examples: codex/gpt-5.4, copilot/claude-sonnet-4.5, openrouter/openai/gpt-5.1. There are no alias modes and no policy routing.",
67:   },
68:   {
69:     title: "Compatibility rule",
70:     body: "Third-party apps work well when they let you set a custom base URL, send a custom API key, and choose an arbitrary model string. If an app hardcodes an upstream endpoint, Gunmetal cannot help there.",
71:   },
72:   {
73:     title: "Storage rule",
74:     body: "Everything stays in local app files. No OS keychain. No hosted relay. No shared cloud vault.",
75:   },
76: ];
77: 
78: export const installSteps = [
79:   "Install Gunmetal with npm: npm i -g @dhruv2mars/gunmetal.",
80:   "Run gunmetal setup. That is the golden path: connect one provider, auth it, sync models, create one key.",
81:   "Run gunmetal start or open gunmetal. The TUI can drive the same flow if you prefer.",
82:   "Call GET /v1/models with your Gunmetal key to confirm the local gateway works.",
83:   "Point your app at http://127.0.0.1:4684/v1 and use a provider/model id like codex/gpt-5.4.",
84: ];
85: 
86: export const changelogEntries = [

88:     date: "March 21, 2026",
89:     title: "Product hardening",
90:     body: "Added real daemon autostart, start/stop/status flows, richer TUI state, local logs commands, and the full landing/docs/install/changelog site.",
91:   },
92:   {
93:     date: "March 2026",
94:     title: "API coverage",
95:     body: "Completed both chat/completions and responses support under one local Gunmetal contract.",
96:   },

98:     date: "March 2026",
99:     title: "Core provider set",

101:   },
102:   {

105:     body: "Set the monorepo, local storage, Gunmetal keys, provider records, registry, lightweight telemetry, CLI, and TUI baseline.",
106:   },
107: ];
108: 
109: export const installSnippet = `npm i -g @dhruv2mars/gunmetal
110: 
111: gunmetal setup
112: gunmetal web
113: 
114: # or keep the daemon running without opening the browser

116: gunmetal status

121: `;
122: 
123: export const apiSnippet = `export OPENAI_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:4684/v1
124: export OPENAI_API_KEY=REDACTED
125: export MODEL=codex/gpt-5.4
126:

143:     "model": "openrouter/openai/gpt-5.1",
144:     "instructions": "be terse",
145:     "input": "summarize the last build",
146:     "gunmetal": { "mode": "passthrough" },

156: # Gunmetal works when the app talks to Gunmetal

(End of file - total 157 lines)

1: @import "tailwindcss";

3: /* ============================================

9: @layer theme {

11:     /* ----------------------------------------

16:     /* Background — pure void */

18:     
19:     /* Surface — gunmetal dark */
20:     --surface: #0a0a0a;

22:     
23:     /* Text hierarchy */
24:     --text: #ffffff;
25:     --text-secondary: #60646c;

290:   .delay-300 { animation-delay: 300ms; }
291:   .delay-400 { animation-delay: 400ms; }
292:   .delay-500 { animation-delay: 500ms; }
293:   .delay-600 { animation-delay: 600ms; }
294:   .delay-800 { animation-delay: 800ms; }
295:   .delay-1000 { animation-delay: 1000ms; }
296:   .delay-1200 { animation-delay: 1200ms; }
297:   .delay-1600 { animation-delay: 1600ms; }
298:   .delay-2000 { animation-delay: 2000ms; }
299: 
300:   /* Initially hidden for animation */
301:   .animate-ready {
302:     opacity: 0;
303:   }
304: }
305: 
306: /* ============================================
307:    KEYFRAMES
308:    ============================================ */
309: 
310: @keyframes fadeIn {
311:   from { opacity: 0; }
312:   to { opacity: 1; }
313: }
314: 
315: @keyframes fadeInUp {
316:   from { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(8px); }
317:   to { opacity: 1; transform: translateY(0); }
318: }
319: 
320: @keyframes fadeInScale {
321:   from { opacity: 0; transform: scale(0.95); }
322:   to { opacity: 1; transform: scale(1); }
323: }
324: 
325: @keyframes wordReveal {
326:   from { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(4px); }
327:   to { opacity: 1; transform: translateY(0); }
328: }
329: 
330: @keyframes typewriter {
331:   from { clip-path: inset(0 100% 0 0); }
332:   to { clip-path: inset(0 0 0 0); }
333: }
334: 
335: @keyframes bounce {
336:   0%, 100% { transform: translateY(0); }
337:   50% { transform: translateY(8px); }
338: }
339: 
340: /* ============================================
341:    COMPONENT UTILITIES
342:    ============================================ */
343: 
344: /* Glass effect — navbar backdrop on scroll */
345: .glass {
346:   background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.7);
347:   backdrop-filter: blur(20px);
348:   -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(20px);
349: }
350: 
351: /* Blueprint grid pattern */
352: .grid-pattern {
353:   background-image:
354:     linear-gradient(var(--grid-color) 1px, transparent 1px),
355:     linear-gradient(90deg, var(--grid-color) 1px, transparent 1px);
356:   background-size: var(--grid-size) var(--grid-size);
357: }
358: 
359: /* Radial glow — accent glow at bottom */
360: .radial-glow {
361:   background: radial-gradient(
362:     ellipse at center bottom,
363:     rgba(13, 116, 206, 0.08) 0%,
364:     transparent 70%
365:   );
366: }
367: 
368: /* Spark effect — CTA inner glow on hover */
369: .spark-hover {
370:   transition: all 200ms var(--ease-out);
371: }
372: 
373: .spark-hover:hover {
374:   box-shadow: inset 0 0 20px var(--cta-glow);
375:   transform: scale(1.02);
376:   background: var(--cta-hover-bg);
377: }
378: 
379: /* Status dot pulse */
380: .status-dot {
381:   position: relative;
382: }
383: 
384: .status-dot::before {
385:   content: "";
386:   position: absolute;
387:   inset: 0;
388:   border-radius: 50%;
389:   background: var(--accent);
390:   animation: pulse 2s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1) infinite;
391: }
392: 
393: @keyframes pulse {
394:   0%, 100% { opacity: 1; transform: scale(1); }
395:   50% { opacity: 0.5; transform: scale(1.1); }
396: }

(End of file - total 396 lines)
</content>

parts.text: Now I have a comprehensive view of the navbar implementation. Let me compile my findings.

## Navbar Implementation Analysis

### 1. Location of Navbar Component(s)

| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `/Users/dhruv2mars/dev/github/gunmetal/apps/web/src/components/layout/Navbar.tsx` | **Main navbar component** (174 lines) |
| `/Users/dhruv2mars/dev/github/gunmetal/apps/web/src/components/site-shell.tsx` | **Alternative site shell** with integrated header/nav (used on inner pages) |
| `/Users/dhruv2mars/dev/github/gunmetal/apps/web/src/lib/site-content.ts` | Navigation content definition (`primaryNav` array) |
| `/Users/dhruv2mars/dev/github/gunmetal/apps/web/src/components/layout/Footer.tsx` | Footer with additional links |

### 2. Components/Libraries Used

**Built-in/Standard:**
- `next/link` - Next.js routing
- `react` (useState, useEffect) - State management
- Inline SVG icons (GitHub icon, hamburger/close icons)

**No external UI libraries** - this is a custom implementation using only:
- Tailwind CSS (via `@import "tailwindcss"`)
- CSS custom properties (design tokens)

### 3. Styling Approach

**Hybrid approach:**
- **Tailwind CSS** via `@import "tailwindcss"` for utility classes
- **CSS Custom Properties** (design tokens) for colors, spacing, and theme values defined in `globals.css`
- CSS classes composed via `className={[...].join(" ")}` pattern

**Key design tokens used:**
```css
--bg: #000000           /* Background */
--text: #ffffff         /* Primary text */
--text-secondary: #60646c /* Secondary text */
--border: #1a1a1a       /* Borders */
--accent: #0d74ce       /* Blue accent */
--radius-sm: 4px        /* Border radius */
```

### 4. Navigation Links/Items

**In Navbar.tsx (Desktop):**
- `/docs` - Docs
- `/install` - Install  
- `/start-here` - Start Here
- GitHub external link

**In site-content.ts (`primaryNav`):**
```ts
export const primaryNav = [
  { href: "/", label: "Home" },
  { href: "/webui", label: "Web UI" },
  { href: "/start-here", label: "Start Here" },
  { href: "/docs", label: "Docs" },
  { href: "/install", label: "Install" },
  { href: "/changelog", label: "Changelog" },
];
```

**Note:** The `primaryNav` in site-content.ts is used by `site-shell.tsx` but NOT by `Navbar.tsx` - there is an inconsistency where the two navigation components have different link sets.

### 5. Key Features of Navbar.tsx

- **Scroll detection** - Adds backdrop blur and border on scroll (`isScrolled` state)
- **Mobile responsive** - Hamburger menu with full-width mobile dropdown
- **Fixed positioning** - `fixed top-0 left-0 right-0 z-50`
- **Glass effect** - Uses `backdrop-blur-xl` when scrolled
- **"use client"** directive - Client-side component with React hooks

### Summary of Relevant Files

```
apps/web/src/
├── components/
│   ├── layout/
│   │   ├── Navbar.tsx      <-- Main navbar
│   │   └── Footer.tsx      <-- Footer
│   ├── site-shell.tsx      <-- Alternative shell for inner pages
│   └── index.ts            <-- Exports
├── lib/
│   └── site-content.ts     <-- primaryNav definition
└── app/
    ├── layout.tsx          <-- Root layout (ThemeProvider wrapper)
    ├── page.tsx             <-- Homepage using Navbar
    └── globals.css          <-- Design tokens & Tailwind
```
